news.summary: Shares of MercadoLibre Inc (NASDAQ: MELI) are experiencing a surge in after-hours trading following the release of their first-quarter results, which surpassed expectations. In Q1, the company reported revenue of $5.93 billion, exceeding estimated $5.51 billion, and an EPS of $9.74, higher than the anticipated $8.03. Total revenue saw a 37% increase year-over-year driven by strategic investments. Commerce revenue rose by 32%, Financial revenue by 37%, and Fintech revenue growth . . . The complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: MercadoLibre Stock Soars with Q1 Earnings Beat: Revenue $5.93B, EPS $9.74; Total revenue up 37% YoY.

news.summary: Visa, the well-known payments company, has recently made a strategic investment in BVNK, a startup focusing on stablecoin infrastructure. This marks Visa's second venture into the crypto world in a short span of time.
news.headline: Visa invests in stablecoin startup BVNK, marking its second crypto move. Payment titan shows confidence in growing demand.

news.summary: Sierra Metals Inc. has released its consolidated financial results for the first quarter of 2025. The company's stock is listed as TSX: SMT and OTCQX: SMTSF, with BVL: SMT also available. The report includes details from the Q1 2025 financial statements and Management’s Discussion and Analysis, which can be found on Sierra Metals’ official website and on SEDAR+ under the Company’s profile. For more information and specific numbers, it is recommended to refer to the aforementioned sources.
news.headline: Sierra Metals reports Q1 2025 financial results. Details available on their website and SEDAR. TSX: SMT | OTCQX: SMTSF | BVL: SMT.

news.summary: Crown Capital Partners Inc. released its financial results for Q1 2025, reporting a net loss of $(1.8) million compared to $(0.6) million in Q1 2024. Adjusted EBITDA was $1.7 million in Q1 2025, an increase from $1.3 million in Q1 2024. Distribution services revenue saw a significant increase to $10.8 million in Q1 2025 from $8.2 million in Q1 2024. Network services revenue also increased to $8.7 million in Q1 2025 from $6.7 million in Q1 2024. Real Estate segment revenue decreased to $1.0 million from $1.6 million in Q1 2024, while Distributed Power revenue decreased to $0.3 million from $0.8 million in Q1 2024. Total equity decreased to $6.9 million from $8.6 million at the end of 2024. Total revenue for Q1 2025 was $21.1 million. More details available on SEDAR at www.sedarplus.ca or Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Crown Capital Partners reports Q1 2025 financial results: net loss of $(1.8)M, Adjusted EBITDA of $1.7M, revenue $21.1M.

news.summary: Robust performance in all three operations resulted in All-In Sustaining Costs coming in below projections and gold production surpassing initial expectations for the first quarter.
news.headline: B2Gold: Q1 2025 results show strong performance - lower costs, higher gold production.

news.summary: On May 7, 2025, Oportun, a financial services company listed on Nasdaq as OPRT, disclosed that Carlos Minetti and Raul Vazquez will be nominated for election at the company’s 2025 Annual Meeting of Shareholders, leading to a reduction in Board members from ten to eight as Scott Parker and R. Neil Williams will not seek reelection. If elected, three of the Board's seven independent directors will have joined within eighteen months of the meeting. After Mr. Williams steps down, a new Lead Independent Director will be chosen by the Board.
news.headline: Oportun to reduce board size from 10 to 8 members, nominate Carlos Minetti and Raul Vazquez for election in 2025.

news.summary: In Palo Alto, California, Fastino AI has introduced a revolutionary AI model structure called ‘TLMs’ - Task-Specific Language Models. Created by AI experts from Google DeepMind, Stanford, Carnegie Mellon, and Apple Intelligence, TLMs provide inference speeds that are 99 times faster than conventional LLMs. These models were trained using less than $100,000 worth of budget-friendly gaming GPUs. Fastino has secured $17.5 million in seed funding led by Khosla Ventures, known for being the initial investor in OpenAI.
news.headline: Fastino launches TLMs, AI models delivering 99X faster inference, trained on <$100K in GPUs. $17.5M seed funding by Khosla Ventures.

news.summary: Robinhood, a brokerage fintech, is reportedly working on a blockchain network to allow European retail investors to trade US securities using tokenized assets like stocks. This move aims to enhance the company's presence in Europe by leveraging blockchain technology. Two crypto firms, Arbitrum and the Solana Foundation, are competing to be partners in the project. Tokenization converts real-world assets into digital tokens for trading on a blockchain, offering benefits like reduced costs, enhanced accessibility, and quicker transactions. Robinhood acquired a brokerage license in Lithuania in April 2025 to operate across the EU and also agreed to buy crypto exchange Bitstamp in 2024. CEO Vladimir Tenev highlighted the speed and power of tokenization compared to the IPO process. Despite a revenue drop of 8.6% in Q1 of 2025, Robinhood exceeded Wall Street's expectations, causing its shares to rise by 2.7% on May 7. No final agreement has been reached with Arbitrum or Solana, according to Bloomberg, but both traditional and fintech firms are exploring blockchain solutions, with examples like Banco Santander and JP Morgan.
news.headline: Robinhood to launch blockchain for European US asset trading, partnering with Arbitrum and Solana. Tokenized securities offer advantages like reduced costs and faster transactions. Robinhood preparing for European market after acquiring Lithuanian license and Bitstamp deal. Revenue fell 8.6% in Q1 2025 but exceeded Wall Street expectations. Robinhood shares rose 2.7% on Nasdaq. No final agreement with partners yet; traditional financiers exploring blockchain solutions. Magazine: Ethereum leading $16.1T TradFi tokenization race.

news.summary: Recently, senior leadership has been bolstered with the addition of skilled individuals boasting substantial industry experience. This move aims to enhance customer satisfaction and bring value to the company.
news.headline: MISTRAS reports Q1 2025 results - new leadership, focus on customer value.

news.summary: Cleveland-Cliffs Inc (NYSE: CLF) disclosed its first-quarter financial results after the market closed on Wednesday. The steel producer reported Q1 revenue of $4.63 billion, falling short of the anticipated $4.64 billion, as per Benzinga Pro. The company reported an adjusted loss of 92 cents per share for the first quarter, missing the predicted loss of 75 cents per share. From March to May, Cleveland-Cliffs made decisions to either fully or partially idle six facilities to optimize its operations, move away from unprofitable ventures, and free up excess working capital. These measures are estimated to yield savings exceeding $300 million. More information is available on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Cleveland-Cliffs reports Q1 revenue of $4.63B, misses estimates. Idles 6 facilities, aims to save $300 million.

news.headline: Altus Group's Annual Meeting had 39,662,907 shares represented, 87.84% of total shares. Voting results disclosed.

news.summary: Allied Gold Corporation (TSX: AAUC) (OTCQX: AAUCF) announced its production results for the first quarter of 2025. During this period, the company produced a total of 84,040 ounces of gold. The total cost of sales, cash costs, and All-in Sustaining Costs (AISC) per ounce sold were reported at $1,838. These figures provide insight into the financial performance and operational efficiency of Allied Gold Corporation in the beginning of 2025.
news.headline: Allied Gold reports strong Q1 2025 results: 84,040 gold ounces produced at $1,838 cost per ounce sold. Optimizing growth projects.

news.summary: On May 8, 2025, Synthetic Grass Warehouse (SGW) will be launching a new distribution center in Houston, TX, providing a convenient location for residents to access high-quality artificial turf products. This expansion is part of SGW's efforts to cater to the landscaping needs of the local community. With the unpredictable weather in Texas, the durable and versatile nature of these synthetic grass products will offer residents a low-maintenance and long-lasting solution for their outdoor spaces.
news.headline: SGW opens new facility in Houston, TX on May 8, 2025, offering durable synthetic turf products for landscaping needs.

news.summary: Innergex Renewable Energy Inc. has agreed to be acquired by CDPQ for $13.75 per share. The acquisition was approved by 99.86% of common shareholders at a meeting. They also achieved commercial operation of the Hale Kuawehi solar facility. Financial highlights for Q1 2025 show an increase in Adjusted EBITDA to $181.9 million. Cash flow from operating activities was $67.9 million. Free Cash Flow for the trailing twelve months was $216.4 million, with Free Cash Flow per share at $1.07. Dividends on common shares amounted to 34% of Free Cash Flow. The corporation is focusing on regulatory approvals for the acquisition and ongoing development projects.
news.headline: Innergex reported Q1 2025 results, including $13.75/share acquisition by CDPQ, reaching commercial operation for solar facility in Hawaii, with $181.9 million in adjusted EBITDA, $1.07 Free Cash Flow per share.

news.summary: The board of directors of The Brink’s Company, based in Richmond, Virginia, announced on May 7, 2025, that they have approved a 5% increase in the regular quarterly dividend on their common stock. The dividend amount has been raised to $0.255 per share. Shareholders of record as of May 19, 2025, will receive the dividend, which is scheduled to be paid on June 2, 2025.
news.headline: Brink’s Company raises quarterly dividend by 5% to $0.255 per share. Payable on June 2, 2025 to shareholders on record May 19.

news.summary: In the midst of economic instability worldwide, the Philippines is emerging as a hotspot for affluent investors. Recognized for its stunning landscapes and inviting culture, the country is attracting attention for its appealing financial benefits including tax incentives, straightforward residency options, and an upscale real estate sector that is currently underrated compared to other global markets. Noteworthy is Manila's remarkable performance in 2023, leading the globe in luxury property price escalation with a significant 26.3% surge.
news.headline: Manila emerges as a global wealth hub with 26.3% luxury price growth in 2023. Business Wire report.

news.summary: On May 7, 2025, in Munich, Envision Energy revealed the release of the EN 8 Pro 8MWh DC Liquid-Cooled Energy Storage System at Smarter E Europe 2025. This cutting-edge technology aims to drive the shift towards a smarter and more resilient energy landscape. Envision Energy, a prominent player in eco-friendly solutions, introduced this system to address the growing demand for efficient energy storage. The EN 8 Pro offers a storage capacity of 8 megawatt-hours and leverages liquid-cooling technology for enhanced performance. This innovation represents a significant step towards sustainable energy solutions and signifies Envision Energy's commitment to advancing green technology on a global scale.
news.headline: Envision Energy launches EN 8 Pro 8MWh DC Liquid-Cooled Energy Storage System at Smarter E Europe 2025.

news.summary: On May 7, 2025, Sprott Inc. ("Sprott") disclosed the outcomes of its Annual Meeting of shareholders. All proposals outlined in the Management Information Circular from March 18, 2025, were approved during the meeting. Sprott operates under the ticker symbols NYSE/TSX: SII, and the event was held in Toronto.
news.headline: Sprott Inc. reports successful Annual Meeting, all resolutions in Management Circular approved on May 7, 2025.

news.summary: Exciting progress is expected in the treatment of MPS I, with ongoing research leading to innovative therapies. Promising treatments like stem cell gene therapy, IDUA Gene therapy, and Recombinant DNA (including OTL-203, Lepunafusp alfa (JR-171), RGX-111, and Iduronicrin) are spearheading these advancements. These developments signify a potential breakthrough in improving the management of MPS I, offering hope for patients and healthcare providers alike.
news.headline: MPS I market to grow at 8% CAGR from 2025-2034. Advancements in treatments like stem cell gene therapy and IDUA Gene therapy.

news.summary: Surge Energy Inc. reported strong financial and operating results for Q1/25, with production averaging 23,567 boepd, exceeding the budgeted estimate of 22,500 boepd. Adjusted funds flow increased to $80.1 million, up 5% from Q4/24. Cash flow from operating activities grew by 29% to $83.5 million. Surge distributed $13.0 million in dividends and returned $5.0 million to shareholders through share buybacks in Q1/25. Net debt decreased by 17% to $246.0 million. Surge drilled 24 wells in its core areas, focusing primarily on the Sparky and SE Saskatchewan regions. Financial highlights include a 4% increase in oil sales to $157.2 million, and adjusted funds flow increasing by 28% to $80.1 million in Q1/25. Operating netback per boe rose by 10% to $43.08.
news.headline: Surge Energy Inc. reports Q1/25 with production averaging 23,567 boepd and AFF of $80.1 million, representing a 5% increase.
